A Real Story

Olusegun reached out not knowing where to start. Here is what happened.

Olusegun Olawumi is a lecturer in Ondo State. Smart man. Already writing. Already thinking about his career online. But he was stuck, unsure how to build his brand, unsure which direction to go, unsure where his existing skills fit in the digital economy.

He found Sochima. They had a conversation. The kind of conversation this diagnostic replicates, specific, honest, built around Olusegun's actual wiring and skills. Not a generic roadmap. His roadmap.

The result? Olusegun is now positioned as a Brand Storyteller, Content Writer, and Ghost Writer, with a sharp LinkedIn headline, a growing audience, and a clear commercial path built from skills he already had. 4,000 followers and counting.

The Real Problem

The confusion is more expensive than the course you bought.

You know digital income is real. You have seen other Nigerians building it, some from your city, some with a weaker CV than yours. You are not confused about whether it works. You are confused about which part of it works for you.

That confusion is why a graphic design course sits unfinished on your laptop. Why you announced you were going into content creation in January and quietly dropped it by March. Why you have spent money on at least one thing that produced nothing, not because you are lazy, but because the lane did not fit.

Every course, every guru, every YouTube video sells you on what worked for them. Nobody has sat down with your specific degree, your specific personality, your specific available time and money, and said: this is your lane, this is why, and this is what you do in the next 90 days.

The gap between "I know digital income is real" and "I am earning digital income" is not effort. It is direction. And direction is exactly what this diagnostic produces.

Before You Read Further

Let's be clear about what this is not.

It is not a course. No video series to finish, no modules, no certificate to add to a CV nobody reads.
It is not generic advice. It will not tell you "content creation is in demand." Everything in your report comes from your specific intake conversation about your specific situation.
It is not a motivational PDF. It will tell you your wealth conditioning is showing up in a specific way, and name the first move that comes from the truth, not from hope.
It is not a shortcut to income. You will still have to do the work. What changes is that the work will be in the right direction.

What the Diagnostic Is

A private intelligence briefing about your own economic future.

A diagnostic is not a prescription. A prescription tells you what to take. A diagnostic tells you what is actually happening, and then the prescription follows naturally from the truth.

After payment, you receive a link to the DWCP Intake Advisor, a GPT-powered conversation that interviews you across 12 areas: your degree, your cognitive wiring, your previous attempts, your money behaviour, your available time, your actual environment, and where you want to be in three years. It takes 30–50 minutes if you are honest.

Sochima feeds your answers into the DWCP analytical framework. Within 72 hours you receive a personalised written report and a personalised video of Sochima walking you through what matters most for your specific situation.

One participant received this in her Section 3: "Lane A: Clinical Credibility Partner. Entry: ₦40,000 per article at Month 2. At twelve months: ₦200,000 per month. At three years: $3,000 per month." Built from her Physiology degree. She did not know any of that before the diagnostic.
I Want This Clarity, ₦10,000

What You Receive

Two deliverables. One clear direction.

📄 Part 1, Personalised Report

5,500–7,500 words across 16 sections. Not a template, every line references answers from your specific intake conversation. Covers your skill lane, income ceiling at 6/12/36 months, wealth conditioning, 90-day build path, your first 30-day moves, and the trap most likely to derail your profile.

🎥 Part 2, Personalised Video

20–30 minutes. Sochima's face on screen, your report open, reading through and flagging the 4–5 things that matter most for your situation. The difference between receiving a medical report and having the doctor walk you through it.

See all 16 report sections ▾

Section 1, Where You Actually Are

Your precise position on the Worker-to-Wealth Spectrum and the single thing blocking the next level.

Section 2, Your Wealth Conditioning

Money beliefs inherited from family and community, and the pattern most likely to derail your first 90 days.

Section 3, Your Skill Options

3–5 viable income lanes built from your profile. Income ceilings at 6, 12, and 36 months. Specific AI tools. Real companies to contact by name.

Section 4, Your Strengths Amplified

3–4 specific strengths from your conversation, each with one action that converts it into a commercial asset.

Section 5, Your Weakness Map

Specific weaknesses with lane-specific consequences. A real book, a specific drill, a relevant community for each one.

Section 6, Degree Leverage vs. Moat

Whether your degree should be front-facing or held as an invisible quality advantage. The exact words for your LinkedIn headline.

Section 7, Your MEAT Audit

How you are deploying Money, Energy, Attention, Time. The primary leak. One specific reallocation move for this week.

Section 8, The Leverage You're Not Using

The highest-value element of your backstory your current path is completely wasting, and how it raises your pricing power 5–10x.

Section 9, Your Credibility Gap

The gap between what you can do and what the market currently sees. One high-leverage action to close it in 30 days.

Section 10, Your Mindset Rewire

Your Cashflow Quadrant position. The identity shift required before your first client meeting.

Section 11, Your Offer Design

Generic vs. positioned offer, side by side. Three-tier packaging: entry service, productized offer, B2B high-ticket.

Section 12, Your 90-Day Skill Build Path

Three phases with specific books, publications to study, companies named by name. NYSC students: your timeline addressed specifically.

Section 13, The Trap Waiting For You

The pattern most likely to take your specific profile sideways, named with evidence from your conversation. The specific counter-move.

Section 14, Your First 30 Days

Three numbered moves. Each specific enough to begin tomorrow with no clarifying questions needed.

Section 15, The Honest Caveat

What the report is uncertain about. The single piece of self-work to do before spending money on the next step.

Section 16, Where This Goes Next

The DWCP Guided Programme and the founding rate available to diagnostic buyers only.

Who This Is For

Read this slowly.

You finished a degree that JAMB chose, your parents chose, or a younger version of you chose without enough information. You know it is not the full picture of what you are capable of.
You know digital income is real. You have tried something, a course, a business, a freelance attempt. It did not go the way you expected. You are not sure if that is because of you or because of the lane.
You are finishing your degree or in your first one or two years of working. You feel the gap clearly. You want a map, not a motivational speech.
You are 27 to 38, you have a job, it pays, and something about the ceiling is visible to you. You know there is an exit ramp. You just do not know which one is yours.
You are in the diaspora. You earn in hard currency. You are starting to realise that "I earn in dollars" is not the same as "I am building wealth."
This Is Me, Start My Diagnostic

Who This Is Not For

The diagnostic is honest. So is this section.

You already have a lane, paying clients, and a positioning statement that works. You need a scaling system, not a diagnostic.
You want someone to tell you that you are doing fine. This report will tell you the truth, specifically and without apology.
You are looking for something to buy instead of something to do. The diagnostic produces a 90-day plan. The plan requires execution. If you are not ready to start Phase 1 within 72 hours of receiving the report, save the ₦10,000 until you are.

The ₦10,000 Question

Why this price. Why now.

₦10,000 is not a lot of money for a personalised intelligence briefing about your own economic future. It is also not free. That distinction is intentional.

It sits below ₦18,000, the lowest-tier affiliate course in the Nigerian digital space, so this is not competing for the same buyer decision. High enough that the real commitment is the 30–50 minute intake conversation, not the price. How you approach that conversation is the first data point about how you will approach everything else.

There is a principle in the DWCP curriculum called the Tourniquet: money that is never deployed toward building anything eventually loses its value to inflation, to stagnation, or to impulse spending. Deploying ₦10,000 into clarity about your own wealth is itself the first wealth-creation move.

What Happens After You Pay

Five steps. Delivered within 72 hours of your conversation.

1

Pay ₦10,000 via Nestuge.

Immediately after confirmation you receive the DWCP Intake Advisor link, your personal GPT diagnostic conversation. No waiting.

2

Complete the diagnostic conversation.

The DWCP Intake Advisor interviews you across 12 areas: your degree, your wiring, your previous attempts, your money behaviour, your time and energy, and where you want to be. Takes 30–50 minutes if you are honest. You can do it in one sitting or return and pick up where you stopped.

3

The analytical framework and video run.

Sochima feeds your compiled conversation into the DWCP framework. The report is reviewed. The personalised screen recording is made, Sochima on screen, reading your report, flagging what matters most for your situation.

4

You receive your report and video.

An email with the Google Doc link and screen recording link. Watch the video first, it tells you which sections to read slowly and which to act on immediately.

5

You begin Move 1 from Section 14 the next morning.

Not next week. Not when you feel ready. The next morning. The first move requires no capital, no existing audience, and no track record, only 90 minutes redirected from something that was not building anything.

One More Thing

The digital skills space is full of people who will sell you a lane without knowing your wiring.

A course that worked for the seller. A business model that worked in a different market. A skill that is genuinely high-income, for someone else.

The DWCP diagnostic does the opposite. It starts with you. Your specific combination of domain knowledge, cognitive style, risk tolerance, available time, and economic situation. And it produces a direction that fits that combination, not one it is hoping fits.

Seventy-two hours. ₦10,000. One report. One video. Everything you need to stop guessing and start building in the right direction.

Rich men negotiate. Middle class bargain. Poor men beg. The first negotiation is with your own clarity about what you are building. This diagnostic is that negotiation made explicit.

Stop guessing. Start building in the right direction.
